characters.name: Vincent Adler
characters.description: Business Man (Former) Vincent Adlerwas a business man and one ofNeal Caffrey's earlier marks. Eight years prior to the show,Mozzieconvinced Neal to run a long con to swindle Adler out of millions of dollars. All they needed was the username and password for Adler's bank account. So Neal infiltrated Adler's business as Nick Halden. This led to the meeting of him andKate Moreau, who was Adler's assistant at the time, andAlexandra Hunter, who was secretly looking into Adler's business but was caught by Neal and Adler. Over time, Adler seemed to trust Neal more and more, even allowing Neal to invest in the business as an employee (which Adler typically never allows) and offering him a raise, which Neal turned down. With Mozzie's constant nagging to get the password, Neal was successfully able to ask Adler for it. However, the day after, news broke out about Adler's sudden disappearance with millions of his clients' money, leaving his employees broke, including Neal and Kate. Checking Adler's account, Neal only found a dollar left in it and realized that the password Adler gave him was an anagram for "Nice try, Neal". Eight years prior to the show,Mozzieconvinced Neal to run a long con to swindle Adler out of millions of dollars. All they needed was the username and password for Adler's bank account. So Neal infiltrated Adler's business as Nick Halden. This led to the meeting of him andKate Moreau, who was Adler's assistant at the time, andAlexandra Hunter, who was secretly looking into Adler's business but was caught by Neal and Adler. Over time, Adler seemed to trust Neal more and more, even allowing Neal to invest in the business as an employee (which Adler typically never allows) and offering him a raise, which Neal turned down. With Mozzie's constant nagging to get the password, Neal was successfully able to ask Adler for it. However, the day after, news broke out about Adler's sudden disappearance with millions of his clients' money, leaving his employees broke, including Neal and Kate. Checking Adler's account, Neal only found a dollar left in it and realized that the password Adler gave him was an anagram for "Nice try, Neal". It was later revealed that Adler escaped to Argentina, which was the original destination of a German U-boat, of which his father was a crewman and who later told Adler about a large amount of Nazi treasure contained in the submarine. Some time later, Adler usesJulian Larssen, his right-hand man, to blackmailGarrett Fowlerinto finding the ambermusic box, which was the key to finding the German Nazi sub. In "Under the Radar", Adler resurfaces, abducting Alex, Neal, andPeter Burketo help him access the submarine, which Adler successfully found but the boat was rigged with explosives to prevent enemy access. Neal and Peter were able to crack the code with Alex's help, revealing the treasure within the U-boat. With no further use for three hostages, Adler drugged them again and subsequently ordered two of his henchman to dro

characters.name: Amanda Callaway
characters.description: Amanda Callawayis a "highly ambitious and pragmatic" former Assistant-Special-Agent-in-Charge assigned to theFBINew York White Collar Division after transferring from the Atlanta White Collar Division. Callaway is put in charge after the forced retirement ofReese Hughes. She likes above-board results, quick high-profile wins, and describes herself as taking an involved approach with her agents. Immediately upon her transfer, Callaway announced that her goal was to make the New York White Collar division "the pride of the Bureau again". In "The Original", Callaway is seen callingSenator Terrence Pratt, alerting him about an evidence box thatPeter Burke,Neal Caffrey, andJames Bennettmay have found in theEmpire State Building. However, she also informs Peter that she doesn't want the evidence box to go to Pratt or Bennett, and would rather the box be given to the FBI first. And though she orders her team to arrest Neal and James on sight during the search, Callaway also commands Pratt to stay where he was instead of joining the search for the evidence box.

characters.name: Diana Berrigan
characters.description: Christie (Ex-Fiance) Diana Berriganis anFBISpecial Agent assigned to the White Collar Division in New York City. Whip-smart and dedicated, Diana Berrigan (Marsha Thomason) is one of the few people in the bureau whomPetertrusts implicitly – which is why it was such a blow when she transferred to Washington, DC for a desk job. Now that she's back in New York, she's part of the action once more, even though the move to the Big Apple is took a toll on her relationship with her girlfriend, Christie. Whip-smart and dedicated, Diana Berrigan (Marsha Thomason) is one of the few people in the bureau whomPetertrusts implicitly – which is why it was such a blow when she transferred to Washington, DC for a desk job. Now that she's back in New York, she's part of the action once more, even though the move to the Big Apple is took a toll on her relationship with her girlfriend, Christie. Still, Diana is glad to be working with her old mentor again, and while she spars withNealfrom time to time, even she would have to admit that the three of them work well together. Diana is the daughter of a diplomat and spent a lot of her childhood living in hotels. She revealed a hotel secret to Neal Caffrey while on a case that behind paintings in a hotel, there can often be a piece of personal art, created by a regular of the hotel room, that creates something to make it feel more like home. Charlie, her bodyguard taught her this. He was killed in the line of duty, protecting her and she has struggled but come to terms with not blaming herself for his death, feeling that is not what Charlie would have wanted. She and Neal then made their own piece of art. (Need to Know) In the Season 1 finale,Out of the Box, Diana comes back from theFBIoffice in D.C. to the New York Bureau where Peter is assigned to help him getAgent Fowlerout of his hair for good. She gets a warrant to search Fowler's computer, but finds nothing of use except an encrypted file. The data on the file self-destructs when she attempts to access it.

characters.name: Jeff Blatnik
characters.description: JB Bellmiere,bornJeff Blatnik,is a "brilliant and volatile avant-guarde artist who studied under a legendary New York sculptor". He apprenticed, and laboured strenuously, under the famous sculptor DuBois. Blatnik grew to feverishly resent the extent to which his late master depended upon Blatnik's own skills and how DuBois's own indecisiveness would manifest itself as more work heaped upon his own shoulders.

characters.name: Jeff Blatnik
characters.description: JB Bellmiere,bornJeff Blatnik,is a "brilliant and volatile avant-guarde artist who studied under a legendary New York sculptor". He apprenticed, and laboured strenuously, under the famous sculptor DuBois. Blatnik grew to feverishly resent the extent to which his late master depended upon Blatnik's own skills and how DuBois's own indecisiveness would manifest itself as more work heaped upon his own shoulders.

characters.name: James Bennett
characters.description: James Bennettis the father ofNeal Caffrey. James was a police officer in the Washington, DC Metro Police Department and his partner wasKathryn Hill. One day, his supervising officer caught him pocketing a stack of cash from a raid. Instead of turning James in, his superior officer offered him a choice: prison or work for the Flynns' (ie, tipping them off, shutting down their competition). All of that came to an end when James finally murdered his supervising officer and was arrested by Kathryn. After a few months, James confessed, turned state's evidence, and was sent to Montana alone as part of Witness Protection. His testimony nearly brought down the whole Flynn Organization. James was a police officer in the Washington, DC Metro Police Department and his partner wasKathryn Hill. One day, his supervising officer caught him pocketing a stack of cash from a raid. Instead of turning James in, his superior officer offered him a choice: prison or work for the Flynns' (ie, tipping them off, shutting down their competition). All of that came to an end when James finally murdered his supervising officer and was arrested by Kathryn. After a few months, James confessed, turned state's evidence, and was sent to Montana alone as part of Witness Protection. His testimony nearly brought down the whole Flynn Organization. After ten years in WITSEC, James returned to DC when the Marshals wouldn't tell him the location of his family. During that time, he got closer to Samuel "Sam" Phelps, an undercover cop and old friend of Kathryn. Years later, James happened to come across Kathryn's email to Sam, so James returned into Neal's life, posing as Sam. He conned Neal,Peter Burke, andMozzieinto helping him find Kathryn's evidence box to prove his innocence, but it only revealed the truth that he was a dirty cop. In "In the Wind", James killedSenator Terrence Prattin self-defense, and left his only witness, Peter, to take the blame.

characters.name: Bianca Esteverena
characters.description: Bianca Esteverenais a convicted Argentinian launderer of stolen items from other thieves. Bianca was raised by her grandfather, a professor who also laundered stolen items, so he taught her the trade. Bianca's auctions are very secretive and carefully organized. Before an auction even happens, Bianca personally vets all clients and sends them a text message with time and location an hour before the auction begins. During the auction, all items are displayed in the main room but surrounded by individually-powered security precautions.

characters.name: Frederick Bilal
characters.description: Frederick Bilalis a Nigerian prince, who shipped crates forJulian Larssenvia his many shipping companies out of the New York Harbor. His vice is horse-racing internationally, spending his days in New York at the Rogers Yacht Club.  Main Characters·Neal Caffrey·Peter Burke·Mozzie·Diana Berrigan·Clinton Jones·Elizabeth BurkeRecurring Characters·Sara Ellis·June·Kate Moreau·Garrett Fowler·Reese HughesCriminals

characters.name: Jim Boothe
characters.description: Jim Bootheis a convicted felon and former thief-for-hire. His M.O. consisted mostly of high-profile smash-and-grabs in Europe. Prior to the show, he was also linked to a ruby heist from the Burj Khalifa in Dubai. In "Diamond Exchange", Boothe pilots the helicopter thatRachel Turnerwas going to use to escape New York. At the end of the episode, Boothe kidnapsNeal Caffrey. In "Borrowed Time", Boothe reveals that he wants Neal to steal the twin of the Hope Diamond back from theFBIso that Boothe could use it as his in with thePink Panthers, instead of getting killed by them since he's seen their faces. However, Neal manages to convince Boothe to forget the diamond and steal something from the Pink Panthers to prove his worth, so they join hands and become partners in the heist. After Neal steals fromAlan Woodford, Boothe tries to turn on Neal but is shot in the shoulder byPeter Burkeand cuffed byDiana Berrigan.

characters.name: Brad
characters.description: Bradis a sexist former stock broker who helped Avery run a boiler room scheme, selling bad stocks to people and making thousands off of them. In "Hard Sell", Brad immediately took a liking to Nick Halden, who he nicknamed "Crazy 8". In discussing their payouts, Brad told Nick that he intends to buy an island.

characters.name: Brooke
characters.description: Brookeis the former assistant of Andrew Stanzler. In "Power Play", Brooke confides inElizabeth Burkewhen she fears her boss is somehow involved in the rolling blackouts across New York.

characters.name: Bugsy
characters.description: Bugsyis the pet dog ofJune Ellington. As part of his rental agreement,Neal Caffreywas tasked with caring for Bugsy whenever June was out of town.  Main Characters·Neal Caffrey·Peter Burke·Mozzie·Diana Berrigan·Clinton Jones·Elizabeth BurkeRecurring Characters·Sara Ellis·June·Kate Moreau·Garrett Fowler·Reese HughesCriminals

characters.name: Bugsy
characters.description: Bugsyis the pet dog ofJune Ellington. As part of his rental agreement,Neal Caffreywas tasked with caring for Bugsy whenever June was out of town.  Main Characters·Neal Caffrey·Peter Burke·Mozzie·Diana Berrigan·Clinton Jones·Elizabeth BurkeRecurring Characters·Sara Ellis·June·Kate Moreau·Garrett Fowler·Reese HughesCriminals

characters.name: Elizabeth Burke
characters.description: Elizabeth Burke(néeMitchell) is the devoted wife of FBI Special AgentPeter Burke. The beautiful, epic, and sophisticated wife of FBI agentPeter Burke, Elizabeth Burke, understands her husband's work ethic. As Peter's friend and mate, she stands by her husband as he tracks elusive criminals and offers fresh perspective whenever he needs it. Elizabeth even gives flirting tricks when her husband is undercover and needs to flirt a woman. Having been married ten years, Elizabeth and Peter's relationship is amazingly strong. Elizabeth even comments at one point that she and Peter "never fight," which is true. The beautiful, epic, and sophisticated wife of FBI agentPeter Burke, Elizabeth Burke, understands her husband's work ethic. As Peter's friend and mate, she stands by her husband as he tracks elusive criminals and offers fresh perspective whenever he needs it. Elizabeth even gives flirting tricks when her husband is undercover and needs to flirt a woman. Having been married ten years, Elizabeth and Peter's relationship is amazingly strong. Elizabeth even comments at one point that she and Peter "never fight," which is true. Elizabeth's support for her husband never wavers, even as she juggles her home life with a demanding career running her own company - Burke Premiere Events.Mozzieat one point claims that he thought Elizabeth's company was merely a front for Peter, and she tells him that sometimes there is no front. Elizabeth seems to have taken a shine toNeal Caffreyand Neal has found some comfort in Elizabeth - she is often a mediator between Neal and Peter when their prior relationship and trust issues threaten their current work together. She is sometimes seen treating Neal like her own son. In thePilotepisode, Peter compiles a list of the things she likes and announces that she likes pottery-making, Nancy Drew mysteries, Oleander-scented candles, old jazz, and anything Italian except anchovies.

In thePilotepisode, Peter compiles a list of the things she likes and announces that she likes pottery-making, Nancy Drew mysteries, Oleander-scented candles, old jazz, and anything Italian except anchovies.

characters.name: Elizabeth Burke
characters.description: Elizabeth Burke(néeMitchell) is the devoted wife of FBI Special AgentPeter Burke. The beautiful, epic, and sophisticated wife of FBI agentPeter Burke, Elizabeth Burke, understands her husband's work ethic. As Peter's friend and mate, she stands by her husband as he tracks elusive criminals and offers fresh perspective whenever he needs it. Elizabeth even gives flirting tricks when her husband is undercover and needs to flirt a woman. Having been married ten years, Elizabeth and Peter's relationship is amazingly strong. Elizabeth even comments at one point that she and Peter "never fight," which is true. The beautiful, epic, and sophisticated wife of FBI agentPeter Burke, Elizabeth Burke, understands her husband's work ethic. As Peter's friend and mate, she stands by her husband as he tracks elusive criminals and offers fresh perspective whenever he needs it. Elizabeth even gives flirting tricks when her husband is undercover and needs to flirt a woman. Having been married ten years, Elizabeth and Peter's relationship is amazingly strong. Elizabeth even comments at one point that she and Peter "never fight," which is true. Elizabeth's support for her husband never wavers, even as she juggles her home life with a demanding career running her own company - Burke Premiere Events.Mozzieat one point claims that he thought Elizabeth's company was merely a front for Peter, and she tells him that sometimes there is no front. Elizabeth seems to have taken a shine toNeal Caffreyand Neal has found some comfort in Elizabeth - she is often a mediator between Neal and Peter when their prior relationship and trust issues threaten their current work together. She is sometimes seen treating Neal like her own son. In thePilotepisode, Peter compiles a list of the things she likes and announces that she likes pottery-making, Nancy Drew mysteries, Oleander-scented candles, old jazz, and anything Italian except anchovies.

characters.name: Peter Burke
characters.description: Peter Burkeis a special agent for the FBI in charge of white-collar crimes. He is also one of the only people that Neal Caffrey truly trusts. A no-nonsense federal agent in the FBI's White Collar Crime Unit, Peter believes in earning success the old-fashioned way, with perseverance and hard work. Adhering to a 'by the book' approach to law enforcement, he has high moral and ethical standards, although havingNealassigned as his CI occasionally makes him more willing to bend rules and look at 'grey areas' for opportunities to catch the real bad guys. Having completed a CBA at college, Peter entered into the FBI's recruitment program not long after. He initially met his now-wife,Elizabeth, during an investigation of a robbery at the gallery where she worked. After putting her under surveillance 'to make sure [she] wasn't seeing anybody else' Peter eventually asked her out, and they have been married for ten years as of the start ofSeason 1. Peter's lacking ability in flirting often puts him in danger while he is undercover and needs to charm a woman. Funnily, it is Elizabeth who usually helps him with flirting with other women (most likely because she knows exactly how deep is Peter's love for her). A no-nonsense federal agent in the FBI's White Collar Crime Unit, Peter believes in earning success the old-fashioned way, with perseverance and hard work. Adhering to a 'by the book' approach to law enforcement, he has high moral and ethical standards, although havingNealassigned as his CI occasionally makes him more willing to bend rules and look at 'grey areas' for opportunities to catch the real bad guys. Having completed a CBA at college, Peter entered into the FBI's recruitment program not long after. He initially met his now-wife,Elizabeth, during an investigation of a robbery at the gallery where she worked. After putting her under surveillance 'to make sure [she] wasn't seeing anybody else' Peter eventually asked her out, and they have been married for ten years as of the start ofSeason 1. Peter's lacking ability in flirting often puts him in danger while he is undercover and needs to charm a woman. Funnily, it is Elizabeth who usually helps him with flirting with other women (most likely because she knows exactly how deep is Peter's love for her). It took him years of diligent investigation, along with the steadfast support of his wife, to finally catch elusive white-collar criminalNeal Caffrey. He has, to date, apprehended Caffrey three times. The second apprehension, after Neal broke out of a maximum security prison, led to the deal being struck that gave Neal some freedom in exchange for wearing a tracking anklet and working for the FBI to catch other white-collar criminals.

characters.name: Byron Ellington
characters.description: Byron Ellingtonis the late husband ofJune Ellington. He was an infamous con man and career criminal, and incarcerated for some time, likeNeal Caffrey. In "Pilot", we learn from June that Byron had great taste in clothes and wore luxury suits and hats. One night, he won a Devore suit from Sy Devore himself in a game of poker. In "Vital Signs", June mentions Byron enjoyed parks for the scenery and fresh air.

characters.name: Neal Caffrey
characters.description: Neal George "Caffrey" Bennettis charming, sophisticated, and is considered to be one of the world's greatestcon artists. Neal was an elusive White Collar criminal, and after a three-year chase, he was eventually apprehended byFBIagentPeter Burke. Extremely crafty and cunning, Neal was only convicted ofBond Forgery, which was just one of the many crimes he was suspected of. But with only four months left on his four-year prison sentence, Neal escapes prison after a visit from his girlfriend,Kate Moreau. He is found once more by Peter, in whom he confides that Kate had left him, and he left prison to get her back. To avoid staying in prison for another four years and to be able to continue his search for Kate, Neal offers to help find the criminal Burke is hunting, as long as he can be released into Burke's custody. After successfully apprehending the criminal, Neal is allowed to serve his remaining four-year sentence on a work-release program as a consultant to the FBI. Neal is known for his good looks, his fitness, his brilliant out-of-the-box thinking, ability to talk, lie or charm his way out of (or into) anything, and charisma. These traits prove to be crucial necessities in his line of work and serve as an advantage in the many instances where his or another person's life is on the line. His zodiac sign is Aries. (Ancient History)

characters.name: Character Appearances
characters.description: A table that shows (in green) which episodes each character appeared in.

characters.name: Christie
characters.description: Christieis a doctor and an ex-fiancee ofDiana Berrigan. They met in pottery class. Some time during their relationship, they moved to Washington, DC, which was difficult for Christie to adjust, so they returned to New York. In "Deadline", Christie invitedNeal CaffreyandSara Ellisto a dinner party at her and Diana's apartment. In "Pulling Strings", Diana announced toPeter BurkeandClinton Jonesthat Christie proposed and they were engaged.

characters.name: Christie
characters.description: Christieis a doctor and an ex-fiancee ofDiana Berrigan. They met in pottery class. Some time during their relationship, they moved to Washington, DC, which was difficult for Christie to adjust, so they returned to New York. In "Deadline", Christie invitedNeal CaffreyandSara Ellisto a dinner party at her and Diana's apartment. In "Pulling Strings", Diana announced toPeter BurkeandClinton Jonesthat Christie proposed and they were engaged.

characters.name: Cindy
characters.description: Cindyis one of the granddaughters ofByronandJune Ellington. Cindy is June's granddaughter and they have a good relationship. As she studies in New York, Cindy is allowed to stay at June's house as she pleases. Neal Caffreyand Cindy's paths cross as both are welcome tenants in June's home. Though Neal finds Cindy very attractive, that's as far as a sexual relationship goes, at least on-screen. But it's understood that they have a mutual friendship, as Neal is able to ask Cindy to help play a small role in distracting a guard in "On Guard".

characters.name: Cindy
characters.description: Cindyis one of the granddaughters ofByronandJune Ellington. Cindy is June's granddaughter and they have a good relationship. characters.name: Kyle Collins
characters.description: Kyle Collinsis a government agent from theOffice of International Affairs(OIA), tasked with bringingNeal Caffreyback to the U.S. after his escape in Season 3. In "Wanted", upon introduction, Collins chastisesPeter Burkefor the lack of information or headway in finding Caffrey. He also hints that Peter knows more than he's leading on, as Peter flagged "E. Parker" after meeting up with Caffrey on Roosevelt Island but neglected to include any E. Parker in his official reports. Collins is later seen speaking withEllen Parkeron her doorstep, but was rebuffed by her.

characters.name: Kyle Collins
characters.description: Kyle Collinsis a government agent from theOffice of International Affairs(OIA), tasked with bringingNeal Caffreyback to the U.S. after his escape in Season 3. characters.name: Lauren Cruz
characters.description: Lauren Cruzwas aFBISpecial Agent, who transferred to the FBI Manhattan White Collar Division from the Organized Crime Division. Beautiful and audacious, Lauren's education quickly establishes herself as a valuable member inPeter Burke's team, as does her predilection for undercover work. Lauren is also very familiar withNeal Caffrey, having written part of her thesis at Quantico about him. Yet, there's an unspoken, yet minimal, amount of tension between them. While Neal wants to play nice, Lauren is dismissive of his talents but also very outspoken when Neal needs to get close to other women, or vice versa, as part of his cover.

characters.name: Lauren Cruz

characters.name: David Pillar
characters.description: David Pillarworks within theFBIand he seemed to hold the key in signing off on the freedom ofNeal CaffreyinSeason 6. In "Return to Sender", with the help ofMozzie, Neal drafted a contract and gave it toPeter Burketo hand to David. The contract stated that Neal would go free in exchange for helping the FBI catch thePink Panthers. With the Pink Panthers being one of the most wanted crime organizations in the world by multiple law enforcement agencies, David had no choice but to get the signature of the Attorney General to ensure that the FBI wouldn't be able to back out of the deal later on.

characters.name: Andrew Dawson
characters.description: Andrew Dawsonis a former prosecutor with theDepartment of Justice, who took a bribe fromCurtis Hagento helpPeter Burkego free. When he found out, Peter forced Dawson to resign.

characters.name: Dekker
characters.description: Karl Dekkeris a former arms dealer who used his flower shop as his cover. He also fenced rare Welsh gold coins forAndrew Dawson, who was referred byCurtis Hagen.  Main Characters·Neal Caffrey·Peter Burke·Mozzie·Diana Berrigan·Clinton Jones·Elizabeth BurkeRecurring Characters·Sara Ellis·June·Kate Moreau·Garrett Fowler·Reese HughesCriminals

characters.name: Zev Demetrius
characters.description: Zev Demetriusis a convicted thief who has historically used his opponents' own heist plans to pull jobs before his opponents have a chance to complete the job, once even getting one over onMozzie. In "One Last Stakeout", out of revenge, Mozzie andNeal Caffreyused Zev's M.O. to their own advantage by acting like Neal was going to steal a painting while their real target was theMosconi Codexdisplayed in the same museum. With Neal going through every step of the theft withPeter Burke, they manage to find Zev's fingerprints on the security gate panel and he was subsequently arrested. Main Characters·Neal Caffrey·Peter Burke·Mozzie·Diana Berrigan·Clinton Jones·Elizabeth BurkeRecurring Characters·Sara Ellis·June·Kate Moreau·Garrett Fowler·Reese HughesCriminals

characters.name: Cole Edwards
characters.description: Cole Edwardsis a former real estate developer and major donor to the campaign ofSenator Terrence Pratt. In "Brass Tacks", Edwards is conned byNeal Caffrey, posing as an architect, into committing fraud by saving millions on low-quality materials for a new building but not reporting the savings to his investors. After his arrest, he rejected an offer byReese Hughesto rat on Pratt, but Pratt threw him under the bus.

characters.name: Cole Edwards
characters.description: Cole Edwardsis a former real estate developer and major donor to the campaign ofSenator Terrence Pratt. In "Brass Tacks", Edwards is conned byNeal Caffrey, posing as an architect, into committing fraud by saving millions on low-quality materials for a new building but not reporting the savings to his investors. After his arrest, he rejected an offer byReese Hughesto rat on Pratt, but Pratt threw him under the bus.

characters.name: June Ellington
characters.description: June Ellingtonis a wealthy widow, and the landlady and friend toNeal Caffrey. June was married toByron Ellington, a con artist who passed away prior to the show. The show doesn't explicitly mention their child(ren), but she has two granddaughters,CindyandSamantha. InSeason 6, she mentions a nephew, so it's safe to assume June has at least one sibling. Though her husband was a career criminal, June loved Byron and misses him very much, often reminiscing of their time together. However, as such, she's not unfamiliar with running cons or afraid of crossing a line or two, even going as far as aiding (ie, offering resources, playing small roles) in some of Neal andMozzie's schemes throughout the series. characters.name: Sara Ellis
characters.description:
Sara Ellisis an insurance investigator withSterling Boschwho had past dealings withNealand is currently in an on-and-off again relationship with him. Her older sister Emily ran away when she was 13, and she never found out what happened to her. Sara's relationship with the con artist is tricky. In the episode, "Unfinished Business", on several occasions, she accuses him of stealing the Raphael painting and tries to get him to admit it. Later in the same episode, she suffers from the effects of Cabin Fever from having been kept inside the bureau since her "death." Neal, trying to warm up to her in order to get the documented "Kate’slast words," invites her to Chinese take-out on the roof. In the following episode, "In the Red", she believes he broke into her home (although it wasMozzie) and has him take a lie-detector test that includes having his apartment searched. However, thanks toPeter, she learns about Kate and sympathizes with him for his loss.  
In "Power Play", she and Neal share some very intense kisses in the archives of Ellis Island, resulting in a relationship that lasts for most of Season 3. She breaks up with him after she found out about the treasure he hides.

characters.name: Eva Perkins
characters.description: Eva Perkinsis the wife ofMozzie. WhileNeal Caffreywas in prison, Eva was target of Mozzie's sweetheart con, only to realize after their marriage that he was also her target. During their honeymoon con in Morocco, Eva ran away with their score and abandoned Mozzie in Marrakech. In "All's Fair", Eva resurfaced, asking Mozzie for a divorce due to her new love,Jack Conroy. Initially, Mozzie didn't want to believe that she had moved on but after much convincing, he decided to let her go until Eva called, asking for his help to steal the Faberge egg so they could run away together. With the help of Neal andPeter Burke, Mozzie was able to see through her con but when she was just about to get caught, Mozzie helped her escape. In the end, Mozzie and Eva didn't end up signing the divorce papers.

characters.name: Maria Fiametta
characters.description: Maria Fiamettais a former art historian and professor for Brooklyn State University. In "Book of Hours", Maria murders mobsterPaul Ignaziofor a valuable 15th-century prayer book.

characters.name: Maria Fiametta
characters.description: Maria Fiamettais a former art historian and professor for Brooklyn State University. In "Book of Hours", Maria murders mobsterPaul Ignaziofor a valuable 15th-century prayer book.

characters.name: Dennis Flynn Jr
characters.description: Dennis Flynn Jrwas a member of the Flynn Organization, an Irish organized-crime family based out of the Baltimore/DC area who had corrupt cops on the payroll, includingJames Bennett. After his father passed away in prison, Flynn tried to revive the family whiskey-counterfeiting business and move into the white-collar world. One day, Flynn was contacted bySenator Terrence Pratt, a former corrupt cop on the payroll, informing him thatJames BennettandEllen Parkerwere responsible for taking down his family. Out of revenge, Flynn tracked down and murdered Ellen and the two Marshals with a specific Browning P9R gun that has been kept in his family for a long time.

characters.name: Dennis Flynn Jr
characters.description: Dennis Flynn Jrwas a member of the Flynn Organization, an Irish organized-crime family based out of the Baltimore/DC area who had corrupt cops on the payroll, includingJames Bennett. After his father passed away in prison, Flynn tried to revive the family whiskey-counterfeiting business and move into the white-collar world. One day, Flynn was contacted bySenator Terrence Pratt, a former corrupt cop on the payroll, informing him thatJames BennettandEllen Parkerwere responsible for taking down his family. Out of revenge, Flynn tracked down and murdered Ellen and the two Marshals with a specific Browning P9R gun that has been kept in his family for a long time.
